thought.text:
The issue is clear. The `SYsULexer.cpp` in task2 only has 15 token types in its `kClangTokens` map. It's missing `const`, `*`, `/`, `%`, comparison operators, logical operators, `if`, `while`, `break`, `continue`, `void`, function call support, etc.

Let me fix all the missing pieces systematically:

reasoning_content:
The issue is clear now. The `SYsULexer.cpp` in task2 has a `kClangTokens` map that only has 15 token types. It's missing `const`, `*`, `/`, `%`, `>`, `<`, `>=`, `<=`, `==`, `!=`, `!`, `&&`, `||`, `void`, `if`, `else`, `while`, `break`, `continue`, `star`, `slash`, `percent`, etc.

The assertion failure happens because the lexer encounters `const` which is not in the `kClangTokens` map.

I need to:
1. Update `SYsULexer.tokens` with all token IDs
2. Update `SYsULexer.cpp` kClangTokens map with all token mappings
3. Extend `SYsUParser.g4` with full grammar (if/while/break/continue/function params/calls/operators)
4. Extend `Ast2Asg` with all missing conversions

Let me also check what tokens the parser grammar uses, and make sure the token IDs match.
